sections.subtitle: Team Form
sections.text: Manchester City are in exceptional form, winning their last 6 matches across all competitions, including a 4-5 thriller against Fulham and a 2-1 victory over Real Madrid. West Ham United, on the other hand, are struggling, with no wins in their last 5 matches, managing only 3 draws and suffering 2 losses, including a 2-3 defeat to Aston Villa.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Manchester City have dominated recent encounters with West Ham, winning both of their last two head-to-head matches with convincing scores of 4-1 and 3-1. The trend of high-scoring games is evident, with over 2.5 goals in all of the last 5 meetings and both teams scoring in 4 of those matches.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Stats
sections.text: Erling Haaland and Phil Foden are in fine form for Manchester City, with Haaland leading the league with 17 goals and Foden contributing 7. West Ham's attack relies on Jarrod Bowen (5 goals), but their defense has been leaky, conceding in all of their last 13 games.

sections.subtitle: Season Overview
sections.text: Manchester City sit 2nd in the Premier League with 34 points, boasting the league's best attack (38 goals). West Ham are languishing in 18th with 13 points, having conceded 32 goals, one of the worst defensive records in the league.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Manchester City's superior form, head-to-head dominance, and attacking prowess, coupled with West Ham's defensive frailties, a comfortable home win is highly likely. The Sofascore model's 87% probability for a home win aligns with this assessment.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Manchester City lead West Ham United by a comfortable 2-0 scoreline at the Etihad Stadium. The home side has dominated proceedings with superior ball possession and numerous entries into the final third and penalty area. Two well-taken goals, one early in the match and another towards the end of the half, have put them in a strong position. West Ham, on the other hand, have struggled to create any meaningful attacking opportunities, failing to register a single shot on target or big chance in the first 45 minutes.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance
halftimeSections.text: Manchester City have been clinical in their approach, capitalizing on their chances with a high number of big chances created, though they missed a couple as well. Their dominance in possession has allowed them to control the tempo of the game, frequently entering dangerous areas of the pitch. Key players like Erling Haaland, who scored and assisted, and Tijjani Reijnders, who also found the net, have been instrumental. The team's ability to win aerial duels and regain possession in the final third has kept West Ham on the back foot throughout the half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance
halftimeSections.text: West Ham United have had a torrid first half, unable to make any significant impact in attack. Their lack of final third entries and penalty area penetrations highlights their struggle to build any meaningful play. Defensively, they've been under constant pressure, conceding multiple big chances and failing to match City's intensity in duels. While they've shown some resilience in ground duels, their inability to retain possession or create chances leaves them with a mountain to climb in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: From the opening whistle, Manchester City took control of the attacking momentum, consistently pushing forward with dangerous spells in the early minutes and maintaining pressure throughout much of the first half. Their attacking intent was evident with frequent waves of pressure, peaking around key moments that led to their goals. West Ham, in contrast, showed very little in terms of forward momentum, with only brief and fleeting moments of attack that failed to sustain or threaten. The momentum has overwhelmingly favored the home side, setting a challenging tone for the visitors in the second half.
